title: Reusable cloth wipe solution
subtitle: A castile-soap and lavender soak for stacked reusable cotton wipes
excerpt: Stack folded cotton cloths in a jar, pour over the solution, and snap the lid shut. Each cloth is pre-saturated and ready to use for surface cleaning, baby wipes, or makeup removal. Castile soap cleans; lavender and tea tree add antimicrobial activity.
sourceNotes: Castile soap surfactant action: standard cleaning chemistry. Lavender and tea tree antimicrobial properties: Cavanagh and Wilkinson (2002); Carson et al. (2006).

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 500 ml jar of solution (enough to soak 15 to 20 folded cloths)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale to fit the jar and number of cloths. The cloths should be moist but not dripping.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh every week; water-based solutions containing plant materials can grow bacteria after a few days. If keeping a jar of soaked cloths, change the solution weekly.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Change the solution and wash the cloths every week. Discard if the solution or cloths develop any sour, musty, or off smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A concentrated plant-oil liquid soap that acts as a gentle surfactant. At the dilution used here it cleans surfaces without leaving a residue on skin.

content.text: Reusable cotton wipes replace paper towels and wet wipes for everyday cleaning. This solution uses

as the base, with lavender and tea tree for cleaning. Use the wipes on kitchen surfaces, bathroom surfaces, and hands. For baby skin or sensitive skin, swap tea tree for extra lavender and use unscented castile soap.

items.prepNote: about 5 ml; keeps the cloths soft

content.text: Combine all ingredients in a measuring jug and stir gently to mix. The solution should be very lightly sudsy -- not thick with soap.

Fold 15 to 20 clean cotton cloths and stack them in a clean jar with a lid. Pour the solution over the cloths. Allow a few seconds for the solution to be absorbed, then turn the jar upside down and back to distribute the moisture evenly. The cloths should feel damp throughout -- press one and it should release a small amount of solution, not drip freely.

Take one cloth from the jar and use it for wiping down surfaces. Used cloths go into a dedicated laundry bag; wash at 60°C with normal washing powder to sanitise them before restacking. Change the solution in the jar weekly, even if cloths remain.

Keep the jar at room temperature in the room where it is used. Change the solution every seven days. Discard immediately if the solution or cloths develop any sour, musty, or off smell -- this indicates bacterial growth and the cloths need a hot machine wash before reuse.
